    <p>{`A keyboard shortcut that can be used to invoke the entrypoint. They are specified separately for Windows and macOS platforms. If the shortcut is not available in the host application, it will be ignored.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`"shortcut": {
    "mac": "Cmd+Shift+P",
    "win": "Ctrl+Shift+P"
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Keyboard shortcuts are defined separately for each platform. Each definition is a string that follows this syntax:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`One or more modifier keys, in any order, each one followed by `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`+`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Mac: modifiers may be Cmd, Ctrl, Opt/Alt, or Shift. The shortcut must contain at least one of Cmd or Ctrl.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Win: modifiers may be Ctrl, Alt, or Shift. The shortcut must contain Ctrl.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`A letter or number key.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Letters are case-insensitive (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"Cmd+P"`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"Cmd+p"`}</inlineCode>{` mean the same thing and neither requires pressing Shift). Other keys (including punctuation, arrow keys, or F1-F12) are currently not supported.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`If your shortcut collides with a built-in command in the host app, or another plugin's shortcut, your shortcut will be ignored, and you'll see a warning printed to the developer console.`}</p>

When not specified, default values will be used.`}</p>
    <p>{`Panel size can be `}<strong parentName="p">{`locked to a specific size`}</strong>{` by setting `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`minimumSize`}</inlineCode>{` equal to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`maximumSize`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>

    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Best practices`}</p>
    <p>{`Make sure you `}<strong parentName="p">{`configure the most accurate permission`}</strong>{` for your use case because in the future we will ask users to provide their consent based on it.`}</p>
    <p>{`For example, for file operations, you may find `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"fullAccess"`}</inlineCode>{` to be the least restrictive and hence the easiest to pick, but a user may not be comfortable giving full access to their system unless it's absolutely necessary; they might deny the installation of your plugin altogether.`}</p>

    <h5 {...{
      "id": "networkpermission"
    }}><inlineCode parentName="h5">{`NetworkPermission`}</inlineCode></h5>
    <p>{`Specifies the domains that the plugin can access in network requests.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "domains": [
    "https://example.com",
    "https://*.adobe.com/",
    "wss://*.myplugin.com"
  ]
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Then, in your plugin code, you can make network requests like this:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Or load images like this:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<img src="https://example.com/image.png" />
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`You can also allow access to all domains by setting `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`domains`}</inlineCode>{` to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"all"`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "domains": "all"
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../resources/recipes/network/index.md"
      }}>{`network recipe`}</a>{` has more details.`}</p>
    <h5 {...{
      "id": "webviewpermission"
    }}><inlineCode parentName="h5">{`WebviewPermission`}</inlineCode></h5>
    <p>{`Enables the plugin to use webviews in its UI to display web content or complex UI.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "allow": "yes",
  "domains": ["https://example.com"],
  "enableMessageBridge": "localAndRemote"
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Then, in your plugin code, you can use a webview like this:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<webview src="https://example.com" />
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`To communicate between the webview and the plugin, you can use the message API:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// In the plugin:
const webview = document.querySelector("webview");
webview.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
  console.log("Received message from webview:", event.data);
  webview.postMessage("Hello from the plugin!");
});

// In the webview:
window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
  console.log("Received message from plugin:", event.data);
  window.uxpHost.postMessage("Hello from the webview!");
});
`}</code></pre>
